title: Air Liquide Egypt, UEG Sign MoU to Develop Green Ammonia Project
content: Air Liquide Egypt and United Energy Group (UEG) have signed a Memorandum of Understanding (MOU) to develop robust and sustainable projects in the energy transition field in Egypt. This partnership aims to assess and develop projects, particularly in the production of low carbon ammonia based on renewable hydrogen. Air Liquide will support UEG in developing renewable-based ammonia production notably by supplying nitrogen to the project and co-developing solutions to mitigate the challenges related to the intermittency inherent to renewable power, to produce competitive ammonia.
author: Analysts Account
